message: Use Auth.authedFetch() instead of raw fetch(). See CLAUDE.md Code Patterns.
Never put secrets in client code. Use environment variables on the server.
Never expose raw error messages in API responses. Use a generic user-friendly message.
Empty catch blocks silently swallow errors. Log or handle the error.
Use const or let instead of var.
Use console.error() for errors in routes. Remove console.log() before committing.
Never set innerHTML with interpolated values — XSS risk. Use textContent or sanitize first.
eval() is a security risk. Find an alternative approach.
Synchronous fs calls block the event loop. Use async fs methods (fs.promises) in route handlers.
Never interpolate values into SQL strings — SQL injection risk. Use parameterized queries ($1, $2, etc.).
Auth middleware is applied per-route, not globally on server.js. See CLAUDE.md API Route Structure.
Migrations are additive. Never drop tables or columns. See CLAUDE.md Database rules.
